title: Beanstalk Governance Flash-Loan Exploit
summary: Beanstalk was drained for roughly $182M after an attacker used flash-loaned capital to acquire temporary governance supermajority, pass a malicious proposal, and immediately execute it through the protocol's emergency path.

classification.privilegeGained: temporary governance supermajority and emergency proposal execution rights
classification.exploitMechanism: malicious governance proposal executed via emergency path after flash-loan-assisted vote capture

response.summary: Beanstalk acknowledged the exploit, disabled governance, pursued community-led recovery planning, and later relaunched with redesigned controls.
actions: reworked governance assumptions after the exploit

attackPath.title: Prepare malicious governance proposal
attackPath.description: The attacker created a proposal that embedded fund-draining behavior behind governance execution.

attackPath.title: Borrow temporary voting power with flash loans
attackPath.description: Massive flash-loaned liquidity was converted into governance influence, giving the attacker the supermajority needed to force approval.

attackPath.title: Execute proposal through emergency governance path
attackPath.description: The emergency execution path allowed the malicious proposal to be committed immediately, draining protocol-controlled assets.

evidence.claimSupported: Beanstalk confirmed the exploit and documented the malicious governance proposal plus emergency execution path used in the drain.
The exploit relied on flash-loan-amplified governance control and one-transaction malicious proposal execution.
Beanstalk lost roughly $182M and became one of the clearest examples of flash-loan governance abuse.

similarIncidentHints: upgrade and admin path abuse through governance
